ocr.text: The data presented in the table offers a comprehensive overview of the commercial applications of Jatobá (Hymenaea
courbaril), particularly focusing on its sap extraction in Campo Grande, MS, Brazil. This region has been identified as a
significant localization for Jatobá harvesting, given its favorable climatic conditions and the abundance of mature trees. The sap
of the Jatobá tree is not only valued for its unique properties but also plays a crucial role in various industries, including food,
cosmetics, and traditional medicine.
Code  Material (vegetal part)  Localization (place, state, country)
Js-1  Jatobá commercial (sap)  Campo Grande, MS, Brazil
Js-2  Jatobá commercial (sap)  Campo Grande, MS, Brazil
Js-3  Jatobá commercial (sap)  Campo Grande, MS, Brazil
Js-4  Jatobá commercial (sap)  Campo Grande, MS, Brazil
Js-5  Jatobá commercial (sap)  Campo Grande, MS, Brazil
Js-6  Jatobá commercial (sap)  Campo Grande, MS, Brazil
Hm-1  Hymenaea martiana (sap)  Serra do Amolar, MS, Brazil
Hs-1  Hymenaea stigonocarpa (sap)  Serra do Amolar, MS, Brazil
Hs-2  Hymenaea stigonocarpa (sap)  Serra do Amolar, MS, Brazil
Hm-sb1  Hymenaea martiana (stem bark)  Serra do Amolar, MS, Brazil
Hs-sb1  Hymenaea stigonocarpa (stem bark)  Serra do Amolar, MS, Brazil
Hs-sb2  Hymenaea stigonocarpa (stem bark)  Serra do Amolar, MS, Brazil
In examining the column labeled "Material (vegetal part)," it is evident that the sap is derived from specific parts of the Jatobá
tree, which are particularly rich in beneficial compounds. Furthermore, the "Code" column categorizes the various products
derived from this material, facilitating efficient tracking and management within commercial supply chains. Understanding the
geographic and material context of Jatobá sap is essential for stakeholders aiming to optimize resource utilization and enhance
market strategies. As demand for natural and sustainable products continues to rise, the insights drawn from this data may prove
invaluable for industry professionals seeking to capitalize on the commercial potential of Jatobá in both regional and global
markets.
